inlinejsr.c

00001 /*
00002  *

00004  * DO NOT ALTER OR REMOVE COPYRIGHT NOTICES OR THIS FILE HEADER

00007  * modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License version
00008  * 2 only, as published by the Free Software Foundation.

00011  * WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of
00012  * MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE. See the GNU
00013  * General Public License version 2 for more details (a copy is
00014  * included at /legal/license.txt).

00017  * version 2 along with this work; if not, write to the Free Software
00018  * Foundation, Inc., 51 Franklin St, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA
00019  * 02110-1301 USA

00022  * Clara, CA 95054 or visit www.sun.com if you need additional
00023  * information or have any questions.
00024  */
00025 
00026 /*=========================================================================
00027  * SYSTEM:    Verifier
00028  * SUBSYSTEM: JSR inlining
00029  * FILE:      inlinejsr.c
00030  * OVERVIEW:  Routines for inlining of JSR and RET bytecodes.
00031  *
00032  *=======================================================================*/
00033 
00034 /*=========================================================================
00035  * Include files
00036  *=======================================================================*/
00037 
00038 #include "check_code.h"
00039 
00040 /*=========================================================================
00041  * Globals and extern declarations
00042  *=======================================================================*/
00043 
00044 /* Maximum byte code size is 64K. */
00045 #define MAX_CODE_SIZE 65535
00046 
00047 typedef struct SubrContext {
00048     int id;                         /* id, for debugging */
00049     int depth;                      /* depth of subroutine */
00050     struct SubrContext *parent;     /* subroutine of caller */
00051     struct CodeRef *caller;         /* jsr that got us there */
00052     struct CodeRef *nextInstruction; /* first instruction following inlining */
00053     int target;
00054 
00055     struct SubrContext *next;   /* linked list of all subr contexts */
00056 } SubrContext;
00057 
00058 /* global context, keep track of info when a method is rewritten */
00059 typedef struct JsrContext {
00060     context_type *vcontext;     /* The verifier's context */
00061     struct CodeRef *codeRef;    /* big array of codeRef's */
00062     struct CodeRef *codeRefEnd; /* pointer to next codeRef to fill in */
00063     int scontext_id;            /* ID assigned to last SubrContext */
00064     struct SubrContext *allSubrContexts; /* pointer to linked list */
00065     struct CodeRef **mapping;   /* maps inumbers CodeRef's */
00066 } JsrContext;
00067 
00068 /* A single instruction in the resulting stream */
00069 typedef struct CodeRef {
00070     long inumber;               /* instruction number in original code */
00071     SubrContext *subroutine;    /* subroutine call that this is part of */
00072     enum { CRF_NORMAL,          /* normal instruction */
00073            CRF_SKIP,            /* skip this instruction */
00074            CRF_JSR_SIMPLE_GOTO, /* jsr to subroutine that doesn't return */
00075            CRF_JSR_TARGETED_GOTO, /* jsr to subroutine that does return */
00076            CRF_RET_SIMPLE_GOTO  /* ret that's not at the end of subroutine */
00077     } flags;
00078     /* My offset in the new code */
00079     int offset;
00080     struct CodeRef *next;       /* next codeRef with same "inumber" */
00081 } CodeRef;
00082 
00083 static bool matchSubroutine(JsrContext *, instruction_data_type*,
00084                             SubrContext *);
00085 static bool subroutineGoto(JsrContext *, SubrContext *, SubrContext *);
00086 
00087 static void
00088 rewriteOneSubroutine(JsrContext *context, SubrContext *subroutine);
00089 
00090 static void fixupCode(JsrContext*);
00091 static void fixupExceptionHandlers(JsrContext*);
00092 static void fixupLineNumberTable(JsrContext*);
00093 static void fixupVariableTable(JsrContext*);
00094 
00095 static void
00096 updateTarget(JsrContext *,
00097              int inumber,
00098              SubrContext* subroutine,
00099              void* target, int offset, int size);
00100 
00101 void
00102 rewriteCode(context_type *vcontext, struct methodblock *mb)
00103 {
00104     JsrContext context_buf;
00105     JsrContext *context = &context_buf;
00106 
00107 #if MYDEBUG
00108     printf("Starting %s.%s%s\n", cbName(mb->fb.clazz), mb->fb.name, mb->fb.signature);
00109 #endif
00110     /* Initialize the context */
00111     memset(context, 0, sizeof(context));
00112     context->vcontext = vcontext; /* The verifier context */
00113     /* Allow up to MAX_CODE_SIZE instructions.  */
00114     context->codeRef = (CodeRef *)malloc(MAX_CODE_SIZE * sizeof(CodeRef));
00115     context->codeRefEnd = context->codeRef;
00116     /* Id (for debugging) of last subroutine structure created */
00117     context->scontext_id = 0;
00118     /* Keep a list of all subroutines, so that we can easily free() them */
00119     context->allSubrContexts = NULL;
00120     /* Make it easy to go from inumber to all CodeRef's that have that inumber*/
00121     context->mapping = (CodeRef **)calloc(vcontext->instruction_count,
00122                                           sizeof(CodeRef **));
00123 
00124     /* Fill in context->codeRef with this routine.  In line all subroutine
00125      * calls, and delete all unreachable code */
00126     rewriteOneSubroutine(context, NULL);
00127 
00128     /* Modify mb->code and mb->code_length for the new code */
00129     fixupCode(context);
00130 
00131     /* Update the exception table */
00132     if (mb->exception_table_length != 0) {
00133         fixupExceptionHandlers(context);
00134     }
00135 
00136     /* Update the line number table */
00137     if (mb->line_number_table_length != 0) {
00138         fixupLineNumberTable(context);
00139     }
00140 
00141     /* Update the local variable table */
00142     if (mb->localvar_table_length != 0) {
00143         fixupVariableTable(context);
00144     }
00145 
00146     /* Clean up */
00147     free(context->codeRef);
00148     free(context->mapping);
00149 
00150     /* Free all the subroutine contexts that we created */
00151     while (context->allSubrContexts != NULL) {
00152         SubrContext *this = context->allSubrContexts;
00153         SubrContext *next = this->next;
00154         free(this);
00155         context->allSubrContexts = next;
00156     }
00157 }
00158 
00159 static void
00160 rewriteOneSubroutine(JsrContext *context, SubrContext *subroutine)
00161 {
00162     context_type *vcontext = context->vcontext;
00163     int depth = subroutine ? subroutine->depth : 0;
00164     instruction_data_type *idata = vcontext->instruction_data;
00165     int instruction_count = vcontext->instruction_count;
00166     CodeRef **mapping = context->mapping;
00167 
00168     instruction_data_type *this_idata;
00169     int inumber;
00170     int count = 0;
00171     CodeRef *retOpcode = NULL;
00172 
00173 
00174     for (    inumber = 0, this_idata = idata;
00175              inumber < instruction_count;
00176              inumber++, this_idata++) {
00177         if (    (this_idata->or_flags & FLAG_REACHED)
00178             &&  (this_idata->register_info.mask_count == depth)
00179             &&  ((depth == 0)
00180                      || matchSubroutine(context, this_idata, subroutine))) {
00181 
00182             /* We have an instruction that is part of this subroutine */
00183 
00184             CodeRef *codeRef = context->codeRefEnd++;
00185 #if MYDEBUG
00186             printf("\t%d:\t%d (%d)\t%s (%d)\n",
00187                    (codeRef - context->codeRef), /* new instruction index */
00188                    inumber, (subroutine ? subroutine->id : 0),
00189                    (this_idata->opcode == 256
00190                          ? "invokeinit" : opnames[this_idata->opcode]),
00191                    this_idata->offset);
00192 #endif
00193             codeRef->inumber = inumber;
00194             codeRef->subroutine = subroutine;
00195             codeRef->flags = CRF_NORMAL;
00196             codeRef->next = mapping[inumber]; /* Add to inumber mapping */
00197             mapping[inumber] = codeRef;
00198 
00199             count++;
00200 
00201             if (count == 1 && depth > 0) {
00202                 /* This is the first instruction included as part of the
00203                  * subroutine call.  If it's the target of the jsr that got
00204                  * us here, then we can just "ignore" the jsr.
00205                  * Otherwise, we have to convert the 'jsr' into a 'goto'
00206                  */
00207                 CodeRef *caller = subroutine->caller;
00208                 if (inumber != idata[caller->inumber].operand.i) {
00209                     caller->flags = CRF_JSR_TARGETED_GOTO;
00210                 }
00211             }
00212 
00213             switch(this_idata->opcode) {
00214                 case opc_jsr: case opc_jsr_w:
00215                     if (this_idata->operand2.i == UNKNOWN_RET_INSTRUCTION) {
00216                         /* We're calling a subroutine that doesn't return.
00217                          * The verifier has already made sure that the
00218                          * subroutine doesn't have a deeper depth.
00219                          * We turn the JSR into a goto */
00220                         codeRef->flags = CRF_JSR_SIMPLE_GOTO;
00221                     } else {
00222                         SubrContext *newSubr = malloc(sizeof(SubrContext));
00223 
00224                         /* In rare cases, we'll have to change this in the
00225                          * subroutine code */
00226                         codeRef->flags = CRF_SKIP;
00227 
00228                         /* Create a new subroutine, and inline it */
00229                         newSubr->id = ++context->scontext_id;
00230                         newSubr->caller = codeRef;
00231                         newSubr->target = this_idata->operand.i;
00232                         newSubr->depth = depth + 1;
00233                         newSubr->nextInstruction = NULL; /* unknown for now */
00234                         newSubr->parent = subroutine;
00235                         /* Add this to the list of all subroutine contexts */
00236                         newSubr->next = context->allSubrContexts;
00237                         context->allSubrContexts = newSubr;
00238                         /* Generate the code for this subroutine */
00239                         rewriteOneSubroutine(context, newSubr);
00240                     }
00241                     break;
00242 
00243                 case opc_ret:
00244                     if (retOpcode != NULL) {
00245                         /* There should only be one per subroutine */
00246                         panic("Multiple return opcodes??");
00247                     } else if (depth == 0) {
00248                         /* We're not in a subroutine */
00249                         panic("Ret at depth = 0");
00250                     }
00251                     retOpcode = codeRef;
00252                     /* Flags are set at the end of the loop, below */
00253                     break;
00254 
00255                 case opc_astore:
00256                     /* We discard any astore's that move a return address
00257                      * from the stack to a register.
00258                      */
00259                     if (GET_ITEM_TYPE(this_idata->stack_info.stack->item)
00260                                         == ITEM_ReturnAddress) {
00261                         codeRef->flags = CRF_SKIP;
00262                     }
00263                     break;
00264 
00265                 default:
00266                     /* Nothing to do */
00267                     break;
00268             }
00269         }
00270     }
00271     if (depth > 0) {
00272         subroutine->nextInstruction = context->codeRefEnd;
00273         if (retOpcode != NULL) {
00274             /* If the last instruction wasn't a 'ret', then we need to
00275              * convert the 'ret' into a 'goto'.
00276              */
00277             if (context->codeRefEnd == retOpcode + 1) {
00278                 retOpcode->flags = CRF_SKIP;
00279             } else {
00280                 retOpcode->flags = CRF_RET_SIMPLE_GOTO;
00281             }
00282         }
00283     }
00284 }
00285 
00286 static void
00287 fixupCode(JsrContext *context)
00288 {
00289     context_type *vcontext = context->vcontext;
00290     instruction_data_type *idata = vcontext->instruction_data;
00291     struct methodblock *mb = vcontext->mb;
00292     unsigned char *oldCode = mb->code;
00293     CodeRef *codeRefEnd = context->codeRefEnd;
00294 
00295     unsigned char *newCode;
00296     CodeRef *codeRef;
00297     int pc;
00298     long newCodeLength;
00299 
00300     /* Assign offsets to each instruction. */
00301 #if MYDEBUG
00302     printf("Assigning offsets\n");
00303 #endif
00304     for (pc = 0, codeRef = context->codeRef; codeRef < codeRefEnd; codeRef++) {
00305         instruction_data_type *this_idata = &idata[codeRef->inumber];
00306         opcode_type opcode = this_idata->opcode;
00307 
00308         codeRef->offset = pc;
00309 
00310 #if MYDEBUG
00311         printf("\t%d:\t%d\tpc=%d\t%s (%d) %s\n",
00312                (codeRef - context->codeRef),
00313                (this_idata - vcontext->instruction_data),
00314                pc,
00315                (this_idata->opcode == 256
00316                     ? "invokeinit" : opnames[this_idata->opcode]),
00317                this_idata->offset,
00318                ((codeRef->flags == CRF_SKIP) ? " XX" : "")
00319                );
00320 #endif
00321 
00322         /* Now increment the pc, depending on the instruction */
00323         if (codeRef->flags == CRF_SKIP) {
00324             /* do nothing */
00325         } else if (opcode == opc_tableswitch || opcode == opc_lookupswitch) {
00326             /* This mysterious calculation works.
00327              * The first term increments pc and then rounds it up to a
00328              * multiple of 4.   The second term is the size of the word-aligned
00329              * values.
00330              */
00331             pc = ((pc + 1 + 3) & ~3) + ((this_idata->length - 1) & ~3);
00332         } else if (opcode == opc_ret) {
00333             /* We must be turning it into an opc_goto */
00334             pc += 3;
00335         } else {
00336             pc += this_idata->length;
00337         }
00338     }
00339 
00340     /* Create a new code object */
00341     newCode = (unsigned char *)malloc(pc);
00342     newCodeLength = pc;
00343 
00344 #if MYDEBUG
00345     printf("Creating code of length %d\n", pc);
00346 #endif
00347 
00348     for (codeRef = context->codeRef; codeRef < codeRefEnd; codeRef++) {
00349         if (codeRef->flags != CRF_SKIP) {
00350             instruction_data_type *this_idata = &idata[codeRef->inumber];
00351             opcode_type opcode = this_idata->opcode;
00352             int pc = codeRef->offset;
00353             unsigned char *source = &oldCode[this_idata->offset];
00354             unsigned char *target = &newCode[pc];
00355 
00356 #if MYDEBUG
00357             printf("\t%d:\t%d\tpc=%d\t%s (%d) \n",
00358                    (codeRef - context->codeRef),
00359                    (this_idata - vcontext->instruction_data),
00360                    pc,
00361                    (this_idata->opcode == 256
00362                          ? "invokeinit" : opnames[this_idata->opcode]),
00363                    this_idata->offset
00364                    );
00365 #endif
00366 
00367             switch(opcode) {
00368                 case opc_ifeq: case opc_ifne: case opc_iflt:
00369                 case opc_ifge: case opc_ifgt: case opc_ifle:
00370                 case opc_ifnull: case opc_ifnonnull:
00371                 case opc_if_icmpeq: case opc_if_icmpne: case opc_if_icmplt:
00372                 case opc_if_icmpge: case opc_if_icmpgt: case opc_if_icmple:
00373                 case opc_if_acmpeq: case opc_if_acmpne:
00374                 case opc_goto: case opc_goto_w:
00375                     target[0] = source[0];
00376                     updateTarget(context, this_idata->operand.i,
00377                                  codeRef->subroutine,
00378                                  target + 1, pc, this_idata->length - 1);
00379                     break;
00380 
00381                 case opc_jsr: case opc_jsr_w:
00382                     target[0] = opc_goto;
00383                     if (codeRef->flags == CRF_JSR_SIMPLE_GOTO) {
00384                         updateTarget(context, this_idata->operand.i,
00385                                      codeRef->subroutine,
00386                                      target + 1, pc, this_idata->length - 1);
00387                     } else if (codeRef->flags == CRF_JSR_TARGETED_GOTO) {
00388                         updateTarget(context, this_idata->operand.i,
00389                                      codeRef[1].subroutine,
00390                                      target + 1, pc, this_idata->length - 1);
00391                     } else {
00392                         panic("Shouldn't have anything referring to jsr");
00393                     }
00394                     break;
00395 
00396                 case opc_ret:
00397                     if (codeRef->flags & CRF_RET_SIMPLE_GOTO) {
00398                         int gotoTarget =
00399                             codeRef->subroutine->nextInstruction->offset;
00400                         target[0] = opc_goto;
00401                         target[1] = (gotoTarget - pc) >> 8;
00402                         target[2] = (gotoTarget - pc);
00403                     } else {
00404                         panic("Shouldn't have anything referring to ret");
00405                     }
00406                     break;
00407 
00408                 default:
00409                     memcpy(target, source, this_idata->length);
00410                     break;
00411 
00412                 case opc_tableswitch:
00413                 case opc_lookupswitch: {
00414                     int *successors = this_idata->operand.ip;
00415                     int keys = successors[0] - 1; /* don't include default */
00416                     SubrContext *subroutine = codeRef->subroutine;
00417                     int i;
00418 
00419                     long *targetPtr, *sourcePtr;
00420                     target[0] = source[0];
00421                     target[1] = target[2] = target[3] = 0; /* clear alignment */
00422 
00423                     targetPtr = (long *)UCALIGN(target + 1);
00424                     sourcePtr = (long *)UCALIGN(source + 1);
00425 
00426                     /* Update the default target */
00427                     updateTarget(context, successors[1], subroutine,
00428                                  targetPtr, pc, 4);
00429                     if (opcode == opc_tableswitch) {
00430                         targetPtr[1] = sourcePtr[1]; /* low */
00431                         targetPtr[2] = sourcePtr[2]; /* high */
00432                         for (i = 0; i < keys; i++) {
00433                             updateTarget(context, successors[2 + i], subroutine,
00434                                          &targetPtr[3 + i], pc, 4);
00435                         }
00436                     } else {
00437                         targetPtr[1] = sourcePtr[1]; /* pairs */
00438                         for (i = 0; i < keys; i++) {
00439                             targetPtr[2 + (i << 1)] = sourcePtr[2 + (i << 1)];
00440                             updateTarget(context, successors[2 + i], subroutine,
00441                                          &targetPtr[3 + (i << 1)], pc, 4);
00442                         }
00443                     }
00444                     break;
00445 
00446                 }
00447             }
00448         }
00449     }
00450     mb->code = newCode;
00451     mb->code_length = newCodeLength;
00452 }
00453 
00454 static void fixupExceptionHandlers(JsrContext *context) {
00455     const int catchFrameSize = sizeof(struct CatchFrame);
00456     context_type *vcontext = context->vcontext;
00457     struct methodblock *mb = vcontext->mb;
00458 
00459     short *code_data = vcontext->code_data; /* maps offset to inumber */
00460 
00461     CodeRef *codeRefEnd = context->codeRefEnd;
00462 
00463     /* Structure to hold new catch frames */
00464     struct CatchFrame *catchFrames = malloc(catchFrameSize * MAX_CODE_SIZE);
00465     struct CatchFrame *currentCatchFrame = catchFrames;
00466 
00467     CodeRef *hRef, *instRef;
00468     unsigned long i;
00469 
00470     /* Look at each exception handler */
00471     for (i = 0; i < mb->exception_table_length; i++) {
00472         struct CatchFrame *this_handler = &mb->exception_table[i];
00473         int start_inumber   = code_data[this_handler->start_pc];
00474         int end_inumber     = code_data[this_handler->end_pc];
00475         int handler_inumber = code_data[this_handler->handler_pc];
00476 
00477         /* First instruction that maps to the specified handler */
00478         for (hRef = context->mapping[handler_inumber]
00479                    ; hRef != NULL; hRef = hRef->next) {
00480             /* Find all instructions that go to this handler. */
00481             bool wasMatch = false;
00482             for (instRef = context->codeRef; instRef < codeRefEnd; instRef++) {
00483                 if (instRef->flags != CRF_SKIP) {
00484                     bool thisMatch = instRef->inumber >= start_inumber
00485                                      && instRef->inumber < end_inumber
00486                                      && subroutineGoto(context,
00487                                                        instRef->subroutine,
00488                                                        hRef->subroutine);
00489                     if (thisMatch && !wasMatch) {
00490                         /* Start a new catch frame */
00491                         memcpy(currentCatchFrame, this_handler, catchFrameSize);
00492                         currentCatchFrame->handler_pc = hRef->offset;
00493                         currentCatchFrame->start_pc = instRef->offset;
00494                         wasMatch = true;
00495                     } else if (wasMatch && !thisMatch) {
00496                         currentCatchFrame->end_pc = instRef->offset;
00497                         currentCatchFrame++;
00498                         wasMatch = false;
00499                     }
00500                 }
00501             }
00502             if (wasMatch) {
00503                 /* We end the code still in the catch frame */
00504                 currentCatchFrame->end_pc = mb->code_length;
00505                 currentCatchFrame++;
00506             }
00507         }
00508     }
00509     /* free(mb->exception_table); */
00510     mb->exception_table_length = currentCatchFrame - catchFrames;
00511     mb->exception_table = realloc(catchFrames,
00512                              (char *)currentCatchFrame - (char *)catchFrames);
00513 
00514 }
00515 
00516 static void fixupLineNumberTable(JsrContext *context) {
00517     context_type *vcontext = context->vcontext;
00518     struct methodblock *mb = vcontext->mb;
00519     int tableLength = mb->line_number_table_length;
00520 
00521     instruction_data_type *idata = vcontext->instruction_data;
00522     instruction_data_type *last_idata = &idata[vcontext->instruction_count - 1];
00523     int oldCodeLength = last_idata->offset + last_idata->length;
00524     struct lineno *lineTable = malloc(sizeof(struct lineno) * MAX_CODE_SIZE);
00525     struct lineno *currentLineTableEntry = lineTable;
00526     unsigned long *mapTable = calloc(sizeof(short *), oldCodeLength);
00527     CodeRef *codeRefEnd = context->codeRefEnd;
00528     CodeRef *codeRef;
00529     int i, currentLineNumber;
00530 
00531     {
00532         unsigned long startPC, endPC, line, pc;
00533 
00534         for (i = 0; i < tableLength - 1; i++) {
00535             startPC = mb->line_number_table[i].pc;
00536             endPC   = mb->line_number_table[i + 1].pc;
00537             line    = mb->line_number_table[i].line_number;
00538             for (pc = startPC; pc < endPC; pc++) {
00539                 mapTable[pc] = line;
00540             }
00541         }
00542         startPC = mb->line_number_table[tableLength - 1].pc;
00543         endPC = oldCodeLength;
00544         line = mb->line_number_table[tableLength - 1].line_number;
00545         for (pc = startPC; pc < endPC; pc++) {
00546             mapTable[pc] = line;
00547         }
00548     }
00549 
00550     currentLineNumber = -1;
00551     for (codeRef = context->codeRef; codeRef < codeRefEnd; codeRef++) {
00552         if (codeRef->flags != CRF_SKIP) {
00553             instruction_data_type *this_idata = &idata[codeRef->inumber];
00554             int thisLineNumber = mapTable[this_idata->offset];
00555             if (thisLineNumber != currentLineNumber) {
00556                 currentLineTableEntry->line_number = thisLineNumber;
00557                 currentLineTableEntry->pc = codeRef->offset;
00558                 currentLineTableEntry++;
00559                 currentLineNumber = thisLineNumber;
00560             }
00561         }
00562     }
00563 
00564     free(mapTable);
00565     mb->line_number_table = realloc(lineTable,
00566                                     (char *)currentLineTableEntry -
00567                                     (char *)lineTable);
00568     mb->line_number_table_length = currentLineTableEntry - lineTable;
00569 }
00570 
00571 static void fixupVariableTable(JsrContext *context) {
00572     context_type *vcontext = context->vcontext;
00573     struct methodblock *mb = context->vcontext->mb;
00574     instruction_data_type *idata = vcontext->instruction_data;
00575     CodeRef *codeRefEnd = context->codeRefEnd;
00576     CodeRef *codeRef;
00577     unsigned long i;
00578 
00579     struct localvar *localVars =
00580         malloc(sizeof(struct localvar) * MAX_CODE_SIZE);
00581     struct localvar *currentLocalVar = localVars;
00582 
00583     for (i = 0; i < mb->localvar_table_length; i++) {
00584         struct localvar *oldEntry = &mb->localvar_table[i];
00585         int startPC = oldEntry->pc0;
00586         int endPC = startPC + oldEntry->length; /* inclusive! */
00587 
00588         bool was_matching = false;
00589 
00590         for (codeRef = context->codeRef; codeRef < codeRefEnd; codeRef++) {
00591             if (codeRef->flags != CRF_SKIP) {
00592                 instruction_data_type *this_idata = &idata[codeRef->inumber];
00593                 bool is_matching = this_idata->offset >= startPC
00594                                    && this_idata->offset <= endPC;
00595                 if (!was_matching && is_matching) {
00596                     memcpy(currentLocalVar, oldEntry, sizeof(struct localvar));
00597                     currentLocalVar->pc0 = codeRef->offset;
00598                     was_matching = true;
00599                 } else if (was_matching && !is_matching) {
00600                     currentLocalVar->length =
00601                         codeRef[-1].offset - currentLocalVar->pc0;
00602                     currentLocalVar++;
00603                     was_matching = false;
00604                 }
00605             }
00606         }
00607         if (was_matching) {
00608             currentLocalVar->length =
00609                 codeRefEnd[-1].offset - currentLocalVar->pc0;
00610             currentLocalVar++;
00611         }
00612     }
00613 
00614     /* free(mb->localvar_table); */
00615     mb->localvar_table_length = currentLocalVar - localVars;
00616     mb->localvar_table = realloc(localVars,
00617                                  (char *)currentLocalVar - (char *)localVars);
00618 }
00619 
00620 static void
00621 updateTarget(JsrContext *context, int inumber,
00622              SubrContext* subroutine, void* target, int offset, int size)
00623 {
00624     CodeRef *codeRef;
00625     for (codeRef = context->mapping[inumber];
00626              codeRef != NULL;
00627              codeRef = codeRef->next) {
00628         if (subroutineGoto(context, subroutine, codeRef->subroutine)) {
00629             int value = codeRef->offset - offset;
00630             unsigned char *t = target;
00631             if (size == 2) {
00632                 t[0] = value >> 8;
00633                 t[1] = value;
00634             } else if (size == 4) {
00635                 t[0] = value >> 24;
00636                 t[1] = value >> 16;
00637                 t[2] = value >> 8;
00638                 t[3] = value;
00639             } else {
00640                 panic("Bad value passed for size");
00641             }
00642             return;
00643         }
00644     }
00645     panic("Cannot find value for updateTarget");
00646 }
00647 
00648 static bool
00649 subroutineGoto(JsrContext *context, SubrContext *from, SubrContext *to)
00650 {
00651     if (to == NULL || to == from) {
00652         return true;
00653     } else if (from == NULL || to->depth >= from->depth) {
00654         return false;
00655     } else {
00656         do { from = from->parent; } while (from->depth > to->depth);
00657         return from == to;
00658     }
00659 }
00660 
00661 static bool
00662 matchSubroutine(JsrContext *context,
00663                 instruction_data_type *this_idata,
00664                 SubrContext *subroutine)
00665 {
00666     int depth = subroutine->depth;
00667     int i;
00668 
00669     for (i = depth - 1; i >= 0; --i) {
00670         if (this_idata->register_info.masks[i].entry != subroutine->target) {
00671             return false;
00672         }
00673         subroutine = subroutine->parent;
00674     }
00675     return true;
00676 }
00677
Generated on Mon Mar 14 2016 15:25:28 for @PACKAGE_NAME@ by  1.7.1
